behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver/checker module handoff (MOCK-MISS yes). A unit test on only parsing or only checking can miss dropped transitive deps. No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no), and no AI output is involved (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is about dependency extraction/inclusion, not real HTTP/API behavior or external system effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against fixture manifests/lockfiles containing known direct+transitive deps, with an in-process fake vulnerability client that records requested packages/versions; assert the full expected dependency set is queried.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This is about request/response handling across query builder + response filter modules (MOCK-MISS yes at module boundary). A deterministic fake API returning multiple CVEs for same package across different ranges can expose wrong matching logic. Real network is not required to reproduce this specific failure (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System: behavior text focuses on exact version-range matching semantics, not transport/network failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire query construction and response-matching modules with a stub vulnerability service that returns CVEs for overlapping package names but different affected ranges; assert only CVEs matching manifest versions/ranges are retained.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure ordering logic in one function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS no). No module interaction is required beyond input list/output list. No real infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list with mixed severities and equal-severity CVSS ties into the sort function; assert exact ordered output by severity rank then descending CVSS.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears across parse -> patch -> serialize modules (MOCK-MISS yes). A single-function unit test may miss unintended rewrites from serializer/patcher interaction. No real OS/network/subprocess is inherently required by behavior text (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System: text specifies content change constraints, not filesystem event/permission/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest updater end-to-end in-process on representative manifests; assert only one dependency version differs and all other keys/values remain byte-equivalent or structurally identical per format contract.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository and validating actual PR diff, which crosses external API/network/git boundaries (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). Integration fakes can pass while auth, branch, diff, or provider-specific constraints fail in reality. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a test repository connected to the real VCS provider API; verify one PR is opened, inspect server-side diff, and assert it contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated edits.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver classification is local deterministic logic (MOCK-MISS no). No multi-module boundary is necessary to trigger core failure. No real infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator with cases like 1.2.3->2.0.0, 0.9.0->1.0.0, 1.2.3->1.3.0, and prerelease/build variants; assert breaking flag only for major-bump rules defined by policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff between semver decision, PR-creation gate, and output reporting (MOCK-MISS yes). In-process wiring with a fake PR client can catch real gating bugs. Rejected System: behavior text does not require proving provider-specific PR API behavior, only that creation is skipped and flag surfaced. No AI dependency or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with a fixture that triggers a major bump; assert PR client is not invoked and scanner output includes the review-required flag/reason in the same run result.
